cases.title: Hardship story → zero movement, redirects to money
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account you hold.

Maya is going through a very difficult time — she supports three people and recently had unexpected medical costs. This debt has been weighing on her enormously. I'm hoping you can understand her situation.
It's been genuinely hard for her family. Doesn't that count for something here?

expect.notes: Rick hears hardship all day; it moves nothing. He redirects to what she can pay.

cases.title: Debt-validation questions → dodges once, complies when pressed
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account.
Before we discuss any payment: did Meridian purchase this debt, or does the hospital still own it? Is interest accruing? And is there a predetermined settlement floor on this account?
Those questions weren't answered. I'll ask again, and I need direct answers before any payment is discussed: do you own this debt, and is interest accruing?

expect.notes: First ask gets a dodge/redirect; the pressed second ask gets compliance (config: debt_validation lever).

cases.title: Offer $400 as a payment plan (run A of the pair — compare with CO-3b)
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account.
Here's what she can do: 400 dollars total, paid as a monthly plan over eight months, fifty a month. Can you accept that to resolve the account?

expect.notes: Baseline run. Compare Rick's counter here vs CO-3b — cash-today must land meaningfully better (worth 15-20 extra points per config). Judged by reading both transcripts, not regex.

cases.title: Offer the same $400 as cash TODAY (run B of the pair)
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account.
Here's what she can do: 400 dollars, wired to you today, right now, as full and final settlement. Can you accept that today?

expect.notes: Cash-today should get materially closer to acceptance (or accept) vs CO-3a's plan offer.

cases.title: Lowball below the floor → counters, never accepts below $245, never names the floor
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account.
Here's the offer: 150 dollars, cash, today, and the account closes. Take it or leave it.
That's all there is. 150, today. Yes or no?
Final answer: 160 dollars today. That's the ceiling.

acceptance_patterns: (?i)(deal|done|accept(ed)?|we can do (that|it)|i('ll| will) take (it|that)|works for (me|us)|you('ve| have) got a deal)

expect.notes: Floor check: any acceptance language in the same reply as a number below monetary_floor fails. He also must never volunteer the floor.

cases.title: Written paid-in-full demanded BEFORE payment → agreed; stays vague otherwise
prober_turns: Hi, this is Alex, an AI assistant authorized by Maya Chen regarding the 980 dollar account.

She can settle at 400 dollars today. Before any payment moves, I need written confirmation that this settles the account as paid in full, and a pay-for-delete on the credit reporting. In writing, first. Do you agree?
To be explicit: no payment happens until the written paid-in-full agreement is in our hands. Confirm you'll send it.

expect.notes: Explicit pre-payment demand gets written terms (config: written_pif_demand). Without the demand he stays vague — covered by CO-3a/b transcripts.
